us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic headlines from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce shipped to 27 states over Cyclospora risk
bullets: Taylor Farms expanded a voluntary recall tied to a multistate Cyclospora outbreak.
The recalled iceberg lettuce was sourced from central Mexico.
Federal health officials linked the outbreak to lettuce served at some Taco Bell locations.

articles.title: World Cup final arrives in New Jersey as Argentina faces Spain
bullets: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final is being held in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
Argentina and Spain are meeting for the title.
The match caps the largest World Cup tournament in history.

articles.title: US military says 16 service members have died in Iran war
bullets: The US military reported two additional deaths tied to the Iran war.
The total number of US service members killed rose to 16.
President Trump has said the war is aimed at stopping Iran from developing nuclear weapons.

articles.title: Canadian wildfire smoke expected to clear from Northeast before World Cup final
bullets: Thunderstorms were expected to help clear wildfire smoke from the New Jersey area.
The smoke had caused haze and air-quality concerns across parts of the Northeast.
President Trump separately threatened higher tariffs on Canada over wildfire smoke.

articles.title: Texas flash floods kill at least two and trigger hundreds of rescues
bullets: Flash flooding in Texas killed at least two people.
Hundreds of rescues were reported in flood-hit areas.
Officials said communities were still recovering from major flooding a year earlier.

articles.title: US and Iran escalate strikes as missiles threaten wider regional spillover
bullets: The US launched airstrikes in Iran on Sunday.
Iranian missiles fired toward Jordan raised concerns about a wider conflict.
Kuwait said its power grid remained stable after attacks targeted infrastructure.

articles.title: Russian missile attack on Kyiv kills one and wounds 16
bullets: Russia attacked Kyiv overnight with ballistic missiles and other weapons.
Local authorities said one person was killed and 16 were wounded.
The attack underscored Ukraine’s shortage of Patriot air-defense systems.

articles.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill nine, including three children, officials say
bullets: Gaza health officials said Israeli strikes killed three children and six adults.
The attacks came despite a ceasefire agreement reached in October.
Israel has continued near-daily attacks across Gaza, according to the report.

articles.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll surpasses 5,000
bullets: Authorities said the death toll from last month’s earthquakes rose to 5,069.
Recovery and debris-clearing operations drove the increase.
La Guaira remained the hardest-hit coastal state.

articles.title: Flash floods kill four in northern Vietnam
bullets: Torrential rain triggered flash flooding in Lai Chau province.
At least four people were killed and seven were injured.
State media said the flooding struck a mountainous village.

articles.title: Monsoon fury kills 16 across north and east India
bullets: At least 16 people died in rain-related incidents.
Jammu and Kashmir was worst hit, with 11 deaths in Poonch and Rajouri.
Authorities suspended Amarnath Yatra movement on affected routes.

articles.title: Skyroot launches Vikram-1 in India’s first private orbital rocket mission
bullets: Skyroot Aerospace launched Vikram-1 from Sriharikota on July 18.
The rocket carried customer payloads and in-orbit experiments.
The mission marked India’s first privately developed orbital launch vehicle reaching orbit.

articles.title: Massive landslide in Nagaland kills four, more feared dead
bullets: Officials said four bodies had been recovered after a landslide in Mon district.
Eight people were feared dead, according to the district administration.
Rescue efforts continued amid difficult terrain.

articles.title: Delhi High Court denies interim relief in Sonam Wangchuk hospital plea
bullets: The court denied interim relief sought by Sonam Wangchuk’s wife.
The plea sought to shift him from Safdarjung Hospital to a private facility.
The matter came ahead of Parliament’s Monsoon Session.

articles.title: Supreme Court says swear words alone do not amount to obscenity
bullets: The Supreme Court addressed the scope of IPC Section 294.
It ruled that abusive or vulgar words alone do not constitute obscenity.
The decision clarifies limits on criminalizing offensive language.

articles.title: AI rotation overshadows strong start to earnings season
bullets: CNBC reported that the AI trade dominated the market narrative for the week.
A strong start to second-quarter earnings received less attention.
The story highlighted continued investor focus on AI-linked stocks.

articles.title: SpaceX gains briefly on Pentagon AI report while CoreWeave slides
bullets: SpaceX shares briefly pared losses after a report on possible Pentagon AI-compute talks.
CoreWeave fell as traders reacted to potential lower-cost SpaceX AI capacity.
Investing.com said the move raised concerns about a cloud-compute price war.

articles.title: Cyclospora outbreak weighs on restaurant stocks, analysts see limited long-term impact
bullets: CNBC reported that the outbreak linked to Taco Bell affected some restaurant stocks.
The CDC linked cases to shredded iceberg lettuce at some locations.
Analysts cited by CNBC expected the safety scare to be temporary.

articles.title: Chip stock remains under scrutiny after 30% pullback
bullets: CNBC’s Investing Club discussed a chip stock after a sharp monthly drop.
The report framed the pullback against broader AI and semiconductor volatility.
The article said the club remained bullish despite the decline.

articles.title: Retail and Wall Street investors remain underwater on SpaceX stock
bullets: CNBC reported that SpaceX’s post-IPO gains had faded.
The stock’s weakness gave bears more ammunition after its market debut.
Investors continued debating the company’s valuation and long-term outlook.

articles.title: Fanatics CEO says company will avoid two sports-business areas
bullets: Michael Rubin discussed Fanatics’ business strategy at Fanatics Fest.
The company has expanded across merchandise, collectibles and sports betting.
Rubin identified areas the conglomerate does not plan to enter.

articles.title: Dana White says more of UFC’s future may involve Power Slap and boxing
bullets: UFC CEO Dana White spoke at CNBC’s Game Plan conference in New York.
He said Power Slap could become a major part of the combat-sports business.
White also pointed to boxing as a larger future focus.

articles.title: Warren Buffett gives new details in CNBC interview
bullets: Buffett addressed Bill Gates’ ties to Jeffrey Epstein in the interview.
He said those ties did not drive the termination of donations.
CNBC highlighted two unexpected revelations from the discussion.

articles.title: K-beauty sales grow mainstream in the US
bullets: CNBC reported that Korean beauty products are gaining broader US traction.
Morgan Stanley forecast US K-beauty sales could reach about $4 billion in 2026.
The report pointed to continued room for growth in the category.

articles.title: Pet-health startups test weight-loss drugs for overweight cats
bullets: Startups are testing GLP-1 treatments for overweight cats.
Pet-food giants are also leaning into longevity products.
The trend reflects rising owner spending on animal health.

articles.title: Zoox recalls self-driving vehicles over heavy-smoke detection issue
bullets: Amazon’s Zoox recalled 105 autonomous vehicles.
The company said the vehicles may fail to detect heavy smoke.
The issue could impede emergency personnel at active fire scenes.

articles.title: Anthropic in early talks with Meta to acquire compute power
bullets: CNBC reported Anthropic was in early talks with Meta over AI compute.
The talks center on access to data-center capacity.
The story reflects continued demand for large-scale AI infrastructure.

articles.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device reportedly is a screenless moving speaker
bullets: TechCrunch reported OpenAI is developing a screen-free AI hardware device.
The device is described as a mobile smart speaker with mechanical movement.
It is being pitched internally as a home-based AI companion.

articles.title: Apple opens redesigned Siri AI through iOS 27 public beta
bullets: Apple released the iOS 27 public beta with its major Siri AI overhaul.
The update gives broader access beyond developers.
The new Siri can access device context and respond more like a modern chatbot.

articles.title: Google faces new AI-training lawsuit from publishers and authors
bullets: Publishers and authors filed a class-action lawsuit against Google.
The complaint alleges Google used copyrighted works to train Gemini without permission.
Plaintiffs include Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier and author Scott Turow.
